scope.summary: Routes raw transport displays through one DbError envelope mapping: generic connection failures are typed CONNECTION_FAILED, proven lost connections and transport markers are typed TRANSIENT, all retain recovery steps, and raw socket text is suppressed.
out_of_scope: A live D5 killed-session recovery run; this close validates the in-process driver-to-tool-envelope mapping.
Changing A4b retry policy or connection quarantine behavior, which remains owned by the already closed A4b work.
Rendering non-transport Oracle error text; existing typed ORA error envelopes remain unchanged.

entry_points.name: DbError into_envelope tool boundary
entry_points.trace: error::tests::raw_transport_display_never_reaches_an_agent_envelope drives Connect, ConnectionLost, Query, and Execute raw transport inputs through DbError::into_envelope and asserts a typed class, next steps, and absence of each raw display string in serialized envelope JSON.

limitations: No live Oracle killed-session lane is claimed; D5 remains the external recovery proof.
The regression protects raw transport markers at the shared envelope seam, not arbitrary server-side log messages, which are not MCP tool responses.
